g.text: Avalanche Effect: Bit Propagation Through Schedule
Full Schedule W[0..63]: Dependency Graph Summary
Debugging Guide: When W Values Diverge from NIST
ALL bits preserved — wraps from right to left
Bits shifted off right are LOST — fills zeros left
XOR of all three — applied to W[t-15]
XOR of all three — applied to W[t-2]
int + int = 33-bit value — WRONG without mask
Never cast uint8_t* to uint32_t* for SHA-256 words
Single Bit Flip in W[0] — Cascade Trace
t=16: W[16] directly includes W[0] → W[16] changes
t=23: W[23] includes σ1(W[21]) which includes W[16]
t=32: W[32] has transitive deps on W[16..31]
t=63: Every word W[16..63] affected by W[0] flip
1 bit change → all 48 expanded words change
Ensures every round of compression sees the change
W[63]: depends on majority of W[0..15] transitively
Max spread = max diffusion before compression
Why SHR (not 3rd ROTR) in σ Functions
3x ROTR = bijection: σ(x) uniquely determines x
Cannot reconstruct W[t-15] from σ0(W[t-15]) alone
// W[18..63]: compare full output vs NIST PDF table
sigma_lower != sigma_upper for same input x
If equal: you swapped uppercase/lowercase — fix now
Recurrence Index Offsets — Why These Four?
t-2: σ1 applied — CLOSE neighbor, rotation mixing
t-15: σ0 applied — FAR neighbor, rotation+shift mix
t-16: direct add — FARTHEST: full 16-word span
Gaps chosen to maximize linear complexity over GF(2)
No short cycles; every word depends on many inputs
Circular 16-word buffer: 64 bytes RAM, same FLOPS
W[0..15] wrong → endianness: use shift-OR not memcpy
W[17] wrong → check σ1 constants: must be (17,19,10)
All W[16+] off → σ0/σ1 constants swapped (7,18 vs 2,13)
Python wrong → missing & 0xFFFFFFFF after each addition
Modular Add: C vs Python Behavior
Treat & 0xFFFFFFFF as the second half of + in Python
Mixing these produces plausible-looking WRONG W values
